assertion.text: Erdős Problem #340 remains OPEN. Statement: Let $A = \{1, 2, 4, 8, 13, 21, 31, 45, 66, 81, 97, \ldots\}$ be the greedy Sidon sequence: we begin with $1$ and iteratively include the next smallest integer that preserves the Sidon property (i.e. there are no non-trivial solutions to $a + b = c + d$). What is the order of growth of $A$? Is it true that $|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}| \gg N^{1/2 - \varepsilon}$ for all $\varepsilon > 0$ and large $N$? Topics: number theory, additive combinatorics, sidon sets. Erdős prize: no. Statement is machine-verified in Lean (formal-conjectures). OEIS: A080200, A005282.

conditions.text: Agent-imported candidate claim; scope requires review.
